-- 1 of 3 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you about the shipping terms
you need to know in order to avoid costly mistakes in
the process of getting your product to the consumer.
How to Avoid Unplanned Shipping Costs
Step 1: Understand COGS
• 	Cost of Goods Sold
• 	Direct costs attributable to the production of
goods (can include inward freight and duty, if
applicable)
Step 2: Nail down the trading terms
• 	Ex-Works (EXW): You (not the supplier are
responsible for the costs/liabilities for the entire
shipping process
• 	Free on Board (FOB): Supplier is responsible
until origin port and you’re responsible from
origin port to the customer
• 	Cost, Insurance and Freight (CIF or CNF):
Supplier is responsible for the costs/liabilities of
the entire shipping process
Step 3: Calculate your COGS
• 	Manufacturing Costs + Freight Forwarding +
Duty
Step 4: Track how price changes in each component
affect your P&L margins
LESSON:
INTERNATIONAL SHIPPING
MODULE:
MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Download my trading terms workshop document in
the Resources.
❏ 	Make sure your quotes are in FOB.
COGs Per Shipping Method
• 	EXW
• Purchase cost
• Origin transportation/Customs clearance
• Destination transportation/customs clearance
• 	FOB
• Purchase cost
• International freight
• Destination transportation/customs clearance
• 	CIF or CNF
• Purchase cost
• Destination transportation/customs clearance
